zhongli_north.name: Zhongli - North
zhongli_north.story: In 1344, Zhu Yuanzhang was seventeen years old. His hometown suffered a great drought, the crops failed completely, and his father and elder brother starved to death one after another. To survive, Zhu Yuanzhang was forced to leave his hometown and beg for food wherever he could. At this time, he had only a broken bowl in his hands, yet in his heart he carried the determination to change his destiny.

zhongli_south.name: Zhongli - South
zhongli_south.story: The southern part of Zhongli County was where Zhu Yuanzhang once lived; it holds the memories of his childhood. Though his family was now broken and scattered, this land had witnessed his growth. Standing here, Zhu Yuanzhang silently vowed that he would change his destiny and ensure that the people of the realm would no longer suffer such hardships.

huangjue_si.name: Huangjue Temple
huangjue_si.story: With nowhere else to turn, Zhu Yuanzhang came to Huangjue Temple and became a monk. The temple's incense was sparse, and the abbot assigned him to sweep the mountain gate. Every dawn, Zhu Yuanzhang would stand before the gate, gazing into the distance, pondering the meaning of life. Though his life was austere, he never gave up hope.

huaixi.name: Huaixi
huaixi.story: The Red Turban Rebellion erupted, and the realm fell into chaos. Zhu Yuanzhang left Huangjue Temple and joined Guo Zixing's Red Turban Army. With his exceptional courage and strategy, he quickly distinguished himself in the army, was adopted as a foster son by Guo Zixing, and married Lady Ma.

henan.name: Henan
henan.story: Zhu Yuanzhang led his troops on campaigns far and wide, achieving remarkable feats in the Henan region. His army was strictly disciplined and deeply loved by the people. By this time, Zhu Yuanzhang was no longer the begging youth of years past, but a commander whose renown struck awe in all directions.

dingyuan_lvpai_zhai.name: Dingyuan Lvpaizhai
dingyuan_lvpai_zhai.story: Zhu Yuanzhang incorporated the armed forces of Dingyuan Lvpaizhai, greatly increasing his strength. He began to recruit worthy talents, and eminent scholars such as Liu Ji and Song Lian came to join him one after another. Zhu Yuanzhang humbly listened to their counsel and devised a series of strategies for governing the realm and securing the nation.

dingyuan_miao_shan.name: Dingyuan Miaoshan
dingyuan_miao_shan.story: At Dingyuan Miaoshan, Zhu Yuanzhang tightened military discipline and trained his soldiers. He knew well that to accomplish great deeds, he must have an elite army. He personally drilled the troops, leading by example, and the soldiers admired him deeply.

hezhou.name: Hezhou
hezhou.story: Zhu Yuanzhang captured Hezhou and established his own base. He practiced benevolent governance, lightened taxes, and pacified the people. The people of Hezhou lived and worked in peace, and Zhu Yuanzhang's reputation grew ever more resounding.

taiping_fu.name: Taiping
taiping_fu.story: Zhu Yuanzhang captured Taiping Prefecture, further expanding his power. He began to establish his own administration, set up government offices, and enacted laws. By this time, Zhu Yuanzhang already possessed the strength to contend for the realm.

yingtian_fu.name: Yingtian
yingtian_fu.story: Zhu Yuanzhang captured Yingtian Prefecture (present-day Nanjing) and made it his capital. He widely recruited worthy talents, developed the economy, and accumulated strength. Yingtian Prefecture became an important base for Zhu Yuanzhang to contend for the realm.

huizhou.name: Huizhou
huizhou.story: Zhu Yuanzhang advanced into Huizhou and defeated the local separatist forces. He implemented the military farming system, developed agricultural production, and solved the army's provisions problem. The people of Huizhou rallied behind Zhu Yuanzhang one after another, and his sphere of influence expanded continuously.

zhedong.name: Zhedong
zhedong.story: Zhu Yuanzhang advanced into Zhedong and defeated separatist forces such as Fang Guozhen. He gained control of the coastal region of Zhejiang and acquired vital maritime routes. By this time, Zhu Yuanzhang had become one of the most powerful forces in the south.

jinhua.name: Jinhua
jinhua.story: Zhu Yuanzhang captured Jinhua, further consolidating his rule in Zhejiang. He established government offices in Jinhua and implemented new policies. The people of Jinhua lived and worked in peace, and Zhu Yuanzhang's prestige reached new heights.

poyang_hu.name: Poyang Lake
poyang_hu.story: The Battle of Poyang Lake erupted, and Zhu Yuanzhang faced off against Chen Youliang in a decisive clash. After thirty-six days of fierce combat, Zhu Yuanzhang triumphed over Chen Youliang despite being outnumbered. This battle laid the foundation for Zhu Yuanzhang to unify the Jiangnan region.

suzhou.name: Suzhou
suzhou.story: Zhu Yuanzhang captured Suzhou and eliminated the forces of Zhang Shicheng. He pacified the people of Suzhou and restored the economy. The prosperity of Suzhou gradually returned, and Zhu Yuanzhang's rule grew ever more secure.

yuan_dadu.name: Yuan Dadu
yuan_dadu.story: In 1368, Zhu Yuanzhang proclaimed himself emperor in Yingtian Prefecture, established the Great Ming Dynasty, and adopted the reign title Hongwu. He dispatched Xu Da and Chang Yuchun to lead a great army on the northern campaign, capturing Yuan Dadu and overthrowing the Yuan Dynasty's rule. Emperor Shun of Yuan fled north in panic, and the Yuan Dynasty perished.

sichuan.name: Sichuan
sichuan.story: Zhu Yuanzhang dispatched a great army to attack Sichuan and eliminated the separatist forces of Ming Yuzhen. Sichuan was incorporated into the territory of the Great Ming, and Zhu Yuanzhang completed the unification of the southwest. By this time, Zhu Yuanzhang had already brought all of southern China under his control.

yunnan.name: Yunnan
yunnan.story: Zhu Yuanzhang dispatched Mu Ying to lead the army in attacking Yunnan and eliminated the forces of Prince Liang. Yunnan was incorporated into the territory of the Great Ming, and Zhu Yuanzhang completed the unification of the entire nation. From a begging youth to Emperor of the Great Ming, Zhu Yuanzhang spent thirty-eight years writing a legendary tale.
